methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Late Stage → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.2x) to $30.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + similar-valuation-tier peers in Space & Aerospace. Range: $6.3B - $37.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 45.3x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Vast
private.rationale: Same sector (Space & Aerospace), same stage (Late Stage), post-money $2B

data.narrative:
Blue Origin, a late-stage Space & Aerospace company, was last valued at $30.0B post-money following $10B+ in total fundraising. The valuation analysis triangulates across a comparable universe of 5 public peers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private participants in the sector.

Two methods were applied. Stage-Progression, which applies a 1.2x step-up multiplier typical of late-stage advancement, implies $36.0B. Peer-Median analysis across 3 same-sector comparables—Rocket Lab ($37.0B), Planet Labs ($7.0B), and Firefly Aerospace ($6.3B)—yields a median of $7.0B, with a range of $6.3B to $37.0B. Public-Comp Anchoring was not applied; while the Space & Aerospace sector median trades at 45.3x price-to-sales across the 6-ticker universe, the target's revenue remains undisclosed. 

The triangulation supports a valuation range of $6.0B to $41.4B, with a median of $21.5B. The wide spread reflects material dispersion in late-stage aerospace comps and the absence of revenue-based anchoring. The $30.0B last post-money sits within this range but above the median, suggesting either premium positioning relative to peers or asymmetric risk reflected in the comparable set. Key uncertainties include unmeasured revenue run-rate, margin trajectory, and the valuation volatility evident in the public peer group.
